docs: ADR 014 — Platform-Native Service Registration

I documented the platform-native service registration strategy
for the Naked Time background service daemon, covering:

- macOS: launchd LaunchAgents with launchctl load/unload
- Linux: systemd user units with systemctl --user
- Windows: Task Scheduler via schtasks.exe /SC ONLOGON

I also updated the ADR index in README.md and replaced the
*(planned)* placeholder in ADR 013 with a proper hyperlink.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mfcollins3 <104274+mfcollins3@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add ADR 012 background service architecture

I created ADR 012 documenting the decision to implement a background
service architecture for Naked Time. The ADR covers the motivation
for moving from a stateless CLI to a CLI/service split, the six key
decisions (single-binary service mode, user-scoped process, CLI/service
separation, IPC transport selection, lifecycle management, and child
process framework), alternatives considered, an architecture diagram,
and the consequences of the decision.

I also updated docs/adrs/README.md to add ADR 012 to the index.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: mfcollins3 <104274+mfcollins3@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial scaffolding: SwiftUI MVVM app with full project structure

- SwiftUI app with 4 tabs: Weekly Releases, Collection, Recommendations, Search
- SwiftData models: Comic, Series, Creator, UserReview, ReadingStatus
- Service layer: ComicVineService, ClaudeService, WebSearchService, RecommendationEngine
- All services protocol-based for testability with mock implementations
- ViewModels: WeeklyReleases, ComicDetail, Collection, Recommendations, Search
- Views: Shared components (CoverImage, RatingBadge, StatusPicker)
- Utilities: APIKeyManager (Keychain), DateFormatters, ImageCache
- 16 unit tests (Swift Testing) + 5 UI tests (XCTest)
- Makefile with build/test/lint/setup targets
- Pre-push git hook (build + unit tests gate)
- XcodeGen project.yml for reproducible project generation
- Agent-ready: .github/copilot-instructions.md with architecture + test commands
- SwiftLint configuration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement CLI tool and Terraform provider

Add the Showbiz CLI (cli/) built with Cobra and the Terraform provider
(terraform/) built with the Terraform Plugin Framework. Both wrap the
Go SDK.

CLI commands: auth, org, project, connection, resource, iam, provider,
config, and shell completion. Supports --output json and --no-color.

Terraform resources: showbiz_project, showbiz_connection,
showbiz_resource, showbiz_iam_policy, showbiz_policy_attachment.
Data sources: showbiz_project, showbiz_connection, showbiz_resource,
showbiz_provider. All resources support terraform import.

Also adds UpdateOrgPolicy to the Go SDK and updates docs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: enterprise rewrite — Advisor Analyzer focused on 'what breaks'

Complete rewrite of Advisor Impact Analyzer for enterprise readiness:

system_prompt rewritten:
- Central question: 'If I apply this fix, what breaks?'
- Mandatory Service Impact Table for EVERY recommendation
- ALL 8 services must appear (positive confirmation for unaffected)
- New columns: Status Atual, Auto-recupera?, Cliente afetado?
- Mandatory dynamic discovery (kubectl) before classification
- Cascade chain required for Low risk and above

dependency-map.md rewritten as Impact Matrix:
- Tabular format: resource × service with concrete impact
- Pre-mapped cascade chains per resource type
- Auto-recovery info per service (replicas, probes)

advisor-impact-playbook.md rewritten with 'O que quebra':
- Per-recommendation service impact tables
- Explicit cascade diagrams
- Post-execution validation checklists
- Critical alert for MongoDB non-root incompatibility

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 3: Rollerblade ground feel + Mario Kart drift state

Rollerblade ground feel:
- Replace instant GroundDeceleration (40) with momentum-based GroundFriction (7)
  and SprintFriction (4) — high-speed momentum glides far, walk-speed stops faster
- Lower GroundAcceleration (52 -> 28) for skate buildup feel
- Crouching movement uses GroundFriction instead of removed GroundDeceleration

Drift mechanic (new PlayerMovementState.Drifting):
- Arm: ExecuteJump sets _driftArmed when sprinting >= 70% of SprintSpeed
- Trigger: on landing, if _driftArmed + strafe held + speed > DriftMinEntrySpeed
- Behavior: movement direction curves toward look forward at DriftTurnRate;
  minimal DriftFriction keeps speed up; _driftCharge builds 0->1 over DriftMaxChargeTime
- Camera: roll increases with charge (DriftMaxRoll * charge * direction);
  FOV lerps from SprintFov to DriftFov at full charge
- Exit (natural): release strafe, drop below speed threshold, or stop sprinting -> Grounded
- Exit (boost): jump while drifting -> ExecuteDriftBoost fires speed burst in movement
  direction * DriftBoostSpeed * (0.5 + 0.5*charge) + normal jump height, enter Airborne
- Fall off edge while drifting -> Airborne

New tuning fields: GroundFriction, SprintFriction, DriftMinEntrySpeed, DriftTurnRate,
DriftFriction, DriftMaxChargeTime, DriftBoostSpeed, DriftMaxRoll, DriftFov

All three presets + DimensionBreachPrototypeBuilder updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: fpslop2
repository.description: Dimensional Breach - momentum FPS movement prototype (Unity 6, no UFPS)

commit.message:
Fix double jump: reset air jumps on ground jump, full-strength air jump, remove jetpack

Root cause: _wasGrounded was set by the second ProbeGround at end of FixedUpdate,
so the !_wasGrounded landing reset in HandleGroundStateTransitions never fired after
the first double jump. Air jumps were silently blocked every subsequent cycle.

Fixes:
- Reset _remainingAirJumps in ExecuteJump() — every ground jump restores double jump
- Remove _jumpReleasedSinceLastJump — no longer needed without jetpack hold mechanic
- Air jump uses full JumpSpeed (was 0.85x) so height is visible
- _inAirJump flag skips JumpCutRate on double jumps so tap-jumps aren't killed early
- Clear all diagnostic Debug.Log calls from jump path
- Jetpack fully removed (energy, Jetting state, fuel bar UI, unlock logic)
- Jump-cut float bug fixed: guard prevents MoveTowards from boosting low velocity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add mandatory per-service impact table to Advisor Analyzer

The most important question for customers is 'what breaks when I
apply this fix?' Now the sub-agent MUST produce a Service Impact
Table for EVERY recommendation showing what happens to each service
during and after the change, with customer impact column.

Includes step-by-step cascade explanation in the output template.

Add tests for large file streaming (upload/download) (#5)

* Initial plan

* feat: add tests for large file handling (streaming, chunking, memory bounds)

Co-authored-by: ruckc <1426401+ruckc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve lint errors and streaming generator upload test failure

- Fix ruff UP043: use Generator[bytes] instead of Generator[bytes, None, None]
- Fix ruff I001: remove extra blank line in import block
- Fix test_upload_streaming_generator: add explicit Content-Length header
  since BaseHTTPRequestHandler doesn't support chunked Transfer-Encoding

Co-authored-by: ruckc <1426401+ruckc@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: apply ruff format to test_large_files.py

Fixes line-length wrapping style and lowercase hex escape sequences
to satisfy `ruff format --check`.

Co-authored-by: ruckc <1426401+ruckc@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: address PR review comments

- Factor out duplicated _make_session() into shared module-level helper
  with explicit requests.Session return type via TYPE_CHECKING import
- Set protocol_version = "HTTP/1.1" on LargeFileHandler so chunked
  Transfer-Encoding is standards-compliant
- Reject POST requests without Content-Length with 411 instead of
  blocking on raw byte reads for chunked requests
- Add server.server_close() and thread.join(timeout=5) to teardown
  to prevent socket/fd leaks across tests

Co-authored-by: ruckc <1426401+ruckc@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add E__product-outlines: portfolio-ready outlines for all five Grey Matter Praxis primary outputs (#20)

* Initial plan

* Add E__product-outlines: six portfolio-ready outlines for clearlineKris

Creates the E__product-outlines/ directory under clearline-regulatory-intelligence/
with a README index and five product outline documents covering the full
ClearLine intelligence stack:

- README.md: index table linking all five product lines
- LOTL-Stack-Outline.md: document architecture, output format, 39-state coverage
- Lil-LOTL-Outline.md: fast-start format, training bullets, upgrade path
- Penumbrant-Pages-Outline.md: two-tier Prose Manual + Executive Briefs
- Field-Issues-Outline.md: ops-vs-regs friction topics, 8-state coverage
- Margin-Issues-Outline.md: policy terrain and gray area topics, 8-state coverage

All content grounded in existing repo source material. Voice matches the
existing authoritative, operator-focused style throughout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add E__product-outlines: portfolio-ready outlines for all five Grey Matter Praxis primary outputs

feat: add Advisor Impact Analyzer sub-agent

Bridges the gap between Azure Advisor recommendations and safe execution.
Analyzes operational impact, maps dependencies, estimates downtime,
classifies risk (Safe/Low/Medium/High), and generates execution plans
with pre-checks, post-checks, and rollback procedures.
